message.thinking:
We need to evaluate the given final text of thesis introduction based on three questions: Chapters, Description, Structure.

We produce JSON array with 3 objects, each containing "question" field set to either "Chapters", "Description", or "Structure", feedback, and complies boolean.

Need to read the text: It's a bullet list under "Analītiskā daļa" (Analytical part) and "Praktiskā daļa" (Practical part). It lists bullet points describing what each part contains.

Question 1: Chapters: Does the text list the main sections of the thesis? The text only lists analytic and practical parts, but not chapters like introduction, methodology, results, conclusion, references. So answer: It does not list all main sections. So fails. Provide feedback: Only analytic and practical part; missing introduction, literature review, methodology, conclusion, etc. Complies false.

Question 2: Description: Does the text briefly describe contents of each section? It does describe contents of analytic and practical parts: bullet lists. So yes, it describes. So complies true. But we can note it's only brief. So feedback: good.

Question 3: Structure: Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text? The text gives two main parts but not overall structure. Hard to get full structure. So fails. Provide feedback: Only two parts, no overview. So complies false.

Let's craft JSON accordingly.
